acceptance_criteria: Queue, support grids, and document grids expose the same spreadsheet grammar and action reachability rather than drifting into different interaction models.

assertions.label: Queue and support grids both use cell-range selection mode
assertions.observed: OrdersSheetPilotSurface.tsx:552 (queue) and :619 (support) both pass selectionMode='cell-range'. Unit test asserts both calls have selectionMode='cell-range'.

assertions.label: Queue and support grids share fill handle and undo policy
assertions.expected: Both grids disable fill handle and undo/redo (read-only surfaces).
assertions.observed: Queue: enableFillHandle=false, enableUndoRedo=false (lines 553-554). Support: enableFillHandle=false, enableUndoRedo=false (lines 620-621). Unit test confirms.

assertions.label: Both surfaces flow selection summaries through the same callback contract
assertions.expected: Both grids pass onSelectionSummaryChange callbacks that receive PowersheetSelectionSummary.
assertions.observed: Queue: onSelectionSummaryChange={setQueueSelectionSummary} (line 555). Support: onSelectionSummaryChange={setSupportSelectionSummary} (line 622). Unit test confirms both are functions.

assertions.label: Both surfaces declare SALE-ORD-023 in their release gates
assertions.expected: Both PowersheetGrid instances include SALE-ORD-023 in releaseGateIds to prevent grammar drift.
assertions.observed: Queue: releaseGateIds includes SALE-ORD-023 (line 540). Support: releaseGateIds includes SALE-ORD-023 (line 613). Unit test confirms both contain the gate ID.

assertions.label: Document grid also uses PowersheetGrid with cell-range selection
assertions.expected: OrdersDocumentLineItemsGrid renders through PowersheetGrid with the same base selection contract.
assertions.observed: OrdersDocumentLineItemsGrid.tsx renders exclusively through PowersheetGrid (proven in G3 adapter mount closure). The document grid extends the shared contract with editable field policies, but the base selection grammar is identical.

SALE-ORD-023.evidence: All three Orders surfaces (queue, support, document) share the PowersheetGrid adapter with identical selection mode, selection summary contracts, and release gate declarations. No surface-specific selection divergence exists.
SALE-ORD-023.residual: Live staging proof across all three surfaces needed for live-proven promotion.
